body:
I see something exactly similar on MacOS / bash / iTerm2

specifically, assuming that I have this function defined
```bash
❯ type bn-xterm
bn-xterm is a function
bn-xterm ()
{
    printf "\033]0;%s\007" "$1"
}
```

then what happens is
```bash
❯ echo $BASH_VERSION
5.3.3(1)-release

# temporary disable 
❯ unset PROMPT_COMMAND

# manually set window title, it shows 'foo'
❯ bn-xterm foo

# calling the hook now as if PROMPT_COMMAND was active
# result is my window title gets replaced by my hostname !
❯ starship_precmd
```

as an aside, the recipe mentioned in https://starship.rs/advanced-config/#change-window-title about defining `starship_precmd_user_func` is not working for me either

essentially all my window titles show my hostname, which is .. a rather serious impediment and not exactly helpful

Any workaround - even hacky ones - would be most welcome :)
